name: Mindful Money Financial Counsel, LLC
description_short: Fee-only financial planning firm in Chicago offering holistic advice on flat fee or hourly basis without investment minimums or commission pressure.
description_long:
Mindful Money Financial Counsel was founded in 2010 by Colin Chase, a CERTIFIED FINANCIAL PLANNER™ with over 25 years of experience in financial services, including 10 years at Fidelity Investments in various roles from service representative to Vice President Senior Account Executive. The firm is independently owned and operates as a small family business based in Chicago, specializing in serving individuals and couples in their 30s and 40s as well as those nearing retirement.

The firm offers comprehensive financial planning and investment advisory services delivered through flexible engagement models: one-time advice sessions or ongoing relationships. Services include holistic financial strategy, retirement planning optimization, tax reduction strategies, college savings planning, emergency fund assessment, investment strategy development, employer benefits analysis, debt prioritization guidance, home affordability evaluation, and life transition planning (marriage, parenthood, career changes, relocation). They provide objective advice without requirements for clients to move assets to the firm or meet investment minimums.

Mindful Money distinguishes itself through several structural features. The fee-only, commission-free compensation model eliminates conflicts of interest inherent in product sales or assets-under-management fees. They explicitly reject the traditional wealth management model of requiring portfolio turnover to the firm. The founding vision emphasizes alignment with health and wellness care models (physician, therapist) where advisors are compensated for value delivered rather than products sold. The firm values trust, optimism, creativity, wellness, sustainability, compassion, humility, and resourcefulness, with marketing explicitly targeting clients seeking validation, second opinions, and objective guidance rather than sales pitches.

Honestly assessed, Mindful Money operates as a legitimate fee-only advisory firm but lacks the depth of regulatory detail, compliance certifications, or third-party credentialing visible on the website. The firm is not positioned as serving low-income or distressed consumers—the target demographic appears to be middle-to-upper-income professionals navigating complexity rather than those in financial crisis. Clients seeking debt relief, credit repair, or emergency liquidity should look elsewhere. The website provides compelling value messaging but limited transparency on specific fee structures beyond "flat fee" and "hourly-as-needed" language.
pros: Fee-only compensation model eliminates commission conflicts and product-sale incentives
No investment minimums or requirements to move assets under management to the firm
Founder holds CFP® credential with 25+ years experience including 10 years at Fidelity
Flexible engagement options: one-time sessions or ongoing advisory relationships
Explicitly targets middle-income households seeking second opinions and validation, not aggressive product sales
Comprehensive holistic planning spanning retirement, taxes, college, debt, and life transitions
Independently owned, small family business structure positioning objectivity over scale
cons: Website lacks transparent fee schedule—only vague references to 'flat fee' and 'hourly-as-needed' pricing
Not positioned for low-income, distressed, or credit-impaired consumers; market is affluent professionals
Limited regulatory or compliance detail visible; no mention of AUM, client count, or firm size specifics
No debt relief, credit repair, or emergency liquidity services for consumers in financial crisis
Heavily personal to founder Colin Chase; unclear succession planning or depth of supporting team (Mary Widman's profile is incomplete)
best_for: Married couples and dual-income households navigating financial optimization and life transitions
Professionals in their 30s-40s seeking holistic planning without pressure to consolidate assets
Near-retirees wanting retirement readiness assessment and tax-efficient withdrawal strategy
Higher-income individuals wanting validation and second opinions on financial decisions
diagnosis: Mindful Money Financial Counsel is best for affluent or upper-middle-income professionals and couples seeking objective, conflict-free financial planning without commission pressure or asset consolidation requirements. The critical caveat is that this is a boutique advisory firm for financial optimization, not a resource for consumers in financial distress, dealing with debt crisis, damaged credit, or needing emergency liquidity—those consumers require different service categories.
services: Holistic financial planning and strategy development
Tax reduction and tax-efficient savings strategies
Emergency fund assessment and adequacy planning
College savings account selection and strategy
Debt prioritization (student loans vs. savings allocation)
Life transition planning (marriage, parenthood, career changes, home purchase)
Cash flow optimization for income changes and life events
One-time advisory sessions and ongoing advisory relationships
Portfolio review without requirement to transfer assets to firm
